summary: Added a first-class read-only clean-stop verifier that checks all three required completion levels: queue state, Git/repo state, and proof/parking-lot state. The queue schema now supports explicit entry_condition values for blocked/deferred slices, queue integrity requires those conditions, and maintained workflow docs point agents to make clean-stop before claiming all actionable work is complete.

contract_status_changes.before: Agents had to combine supervisor dry-run output, Git cleanliness/mirror checks, and manual queue inspection from chat guidance to know whether all actionable slices were complete.
contract_status_changes.after: `make clean-stop` performs the read-only three-level check and fails with remediation-first messages when the repo is dirty, not mirrored, has open queue work, or has parked work without entry conditions.

contract_status_changes.before: Blocked slice entry conditions were encoded only in free-form notes, so a future agent or script could not mechanically distinguish parked work from forgotten work.
contract_status_changes.after: The queue schema supports `entry_condition`, queue integrity requires it for `blocked` and `deferred` slices, and current parked slices carry explicit entry conditions.

residual_risks: `make clean-stop` proves there is no currently actionable queued/in-progress work in the repo state; it does not claim every parked roadmap item is finished or that external prerequisites such as reviewed translations or a fresh TestFlight build exist.
The clean-stop command treats Git mirror status as mandatory for this branch. Repos without an upstream will need to set one or record a separate, explicit not-relevant reason outside this command.
recommended_next_reason: Clean stop. Remaining TestFlight, localization intake, and optional UI regression expansion work is blocked behind explicit entry conditions.
